+

+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference

+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-175_TASK_2026-05-25_12-04-45+0300.md`
+

+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. Audit-only dispatch.
+

+
+- None. Audit-only dispatch.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None. Audit-only dispatch.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Only this EXEC report (and its `.meta.json` sidecar, written by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`) is added by this dispatch. No tracked source / test / fixture / Cargo / lockfile / workflow / script / doc / status / planning / golden-project / handoff / log file was edited or added.
+
+## 5-Question Scene-Loader Consumer Audit Answer Block
+
+### Q1. Current Project/Scene Parse/Load Surfaces
+
+**No non-test parse surface exists** anywhere in tracked code for `rge_data::Project` or `rge_data::Scene`. Every `ron::from_str::<Project>` and `ron::from_str::<Scene>` call site is inside `crates/rge-data/tests/**`, `crates/rge-data/examples/bake_fixtures.rs`, or `crates/rge-scene-loader/tests/**`. There is **one non-test load surface** for `Scene`, owned by the loader crate itself; it never opens a file.
+
+Concrete classification of every surface inspected (non-test surfaces named first, test-only surfaces listed after for completeness):
+
+- **Non-test load surface (Scene → World)** — `rge_scene_loader::load_scene_into_world(&Scene) -> Result<rge_kernel_ecs::World, SceneLoadError>` at `crates/rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs:84-102`. Spawns every entity via `World::spawn_with_id` (line 90-91), then walks each entity's `ComponentValue` list through a four-arm typed `ron::from_str::<T>` match (lines 112-152) for `Transform` / `Camera` / `Light` / `Visibility`. This function never opens a file — it consumes an already-parsed `Scene` value supplied by the caller. It is currently called only from test code (see below); it is not yet wired into any app, runtime, or editor.
+
+- **Schema definitions (NOT parse/load surfaces)**:
+  - `crates/rge-data/src/project.rs:28-44` — `pub struct Project { version, name, description, target_tiers, plugins, scenes }`. Pure schema; no constructor reads a file.
+  - `crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs:33-115` — `Scene` / `Entity` / `ComponentValue` / `Relation` schemas (consulted via re-exports in `crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs:67`). Pure schema.
+  - `crates/rge-data/src/migration.rs` — owns the `migrate(from, to, text)` chain walker; it parses RON via `ron::from_str::<ron::Value>` (`migration.rs:303`, `:312`) but only for the migration-shape check, not as a typed `Project` / `Scene` parser. Not a Project/Scene parse surface.
+
+- **Fixture writer (NOT a parse/load surface)** — `crates/rge-data/examples/bake_fixtures.rs` constructs `Project` / `Scene` / `Prefab` values in memory and calls `ron::ser::to_string_pretty` (lines 29-34) to write them to `crates/rge-data/tests/fixtures/`. It is a writer, not a parser, and it is an `examples/` target rather than a runtime/editor/app surface.
+
+- **Documentation comments (NOT parse/load surfaces)** — `crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs:1-50` (module docstring describing the `.rge-project` / `.rge-scene` / `.rge-prefab` schemas) and `docs/§18/RGE_DATA.md` (design doc). These describe schemas; they execute no parse.
+
+- **Test-only parse surfaces (excluded from the non-test answer, listed only because the Planner notes ask the audit to make the distinction explicit)**:
+  - `crates/rge-data/tests/round_trip.rs:131,141,151` — `ron::from_str::<{Project,Scene,Prefab}>` on tracked fixtures.
+  - `crates/rge-data/tests/schema_validation.rs:130,138,146` — `ron::from_str::<{Project,Scene,rge_data::Prefab}>` on inline fixtures.
+  - `crates/rge-data/tests/migration_test.rs:68,194,230` — `ron::from_str::<{Scene,Scene,rge_data::Project}>` post-migration.
+  - `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:46-150,364-374` — parses `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` + `scenes/main.rge-scene` and runs schema asserts.
+  - `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_load_tick.rs:64-67` — same fixture; parses both and exercises `World::spawn_with_id` + `advance_tick` against an identity-only spawn (no component bridging — pre-dates the loader crate; see [ISSUE-154 EXEC](../ai_handoffs/ISSUE-154_EXEC_2026-05-24_14-59-53+0300.md)).
+  - `crates/rge-scene-loader/tests/simple_scene.rs:34-49` — parses the same golden fixture, hands the resulting `Scene` to `load_scene_into_world`, then asserts entity-level components plus `advance_tick` contract.
+  - `crates/rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs:163-207` — the loader crate's own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (unsupported component, malformed payload, empty scene).
+
+None of these test sites is a valid runtime/editor consumer — they all live under `tests/` or `#[cfg(test)]` and exist to pin the schema + loader contracts.
+
+Workspace-wide grep evidence for "no non-test consumer exists":
+- `Grep "use rge_data" --type rs` returned only the test and `bake_fixtures.rs` sites listed above plus `crates/rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs:35` (the loader's own import).
+- `Grep "rge_scene_loader|rge-scene-loader" --type rs` returned only `crates/rge-scene-loader/{src,tests}/**` and the workspace `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock`.
+- No file under `runtime/**`, `editor/**`, `crates/editor-*/**`, `crates/asset-*/**`, `crates/io-*/**`, `kernel/app/**`, `kernel/asset*/**`, or `kernel/ecs/**` imports `rge_data` or `rge_scene_loader`.
+
+### Q2. Smallest Valid First Consumer
+
+**`runtime/runtime-headless`** (workspace crate `rge-runtime-headless`, declared at `Cargo.toml:125`).
+
+Direction-of-dependency reasoning from manifest evidence:
+
+- `crates/rge-data/Cargo.toml:12-21` lists only `rge-kernel-asset`, `serde`, `ron`, `ulid`, `thiserror` under `[dependencies]` and `rge-kernel-ecs` under `[dev-dependencies]`. **There is no edge to `rge-scene-loader`** here, and the audit explicitly forbids introducing one: the bridge direction is `rge-scene-loader → rge-data`, set when ISSUE-171 created the loader (`crates/rge-scene-loader/Cargo.toml:13` shows `rge-data = { path = "../rge-data" }`). Any `rge-data → rge-scene-loader` edge is rejected — it would invert the schema/bridge tier and force the schema crate to pull in the component crates (`rge-components-{spatial,render,visibility}` per `crates/rge-scene-loader/Cargo.toml:15-17`), defeating the whole purpose of keeping `rge-data` stateless.
+- The first consumer's dep tree therefore flows `consumer → { rge-data, rge-scene-loader, rge-kernel-ecs }`; the consumer parses `.rge-project` / `.rge-scene` via `ron::from_str`, hands the `Scene` to `rge_scene_loader::load_scene_into_world`, and owns the resulting `rge_kernel_ecs::World` directly.
+
+Why `runtime-headless` is the *smallest* valid first consumer (each alternative inspected by reading its manifest + main source):
+
+- `runtime/runtime-headless/Cargo.toml:16-19` declares `[dependencies]` as empty; `runtime/runtime-headless/src/main.rs:1-5` is a four-line stub that prints "stub. Implementation pending per IMPLEMENTATION.md." A headless binary needs neither winit, gfx, audio, nor an editor shell — the loader's output `rge_kernel_ecs::World` can be owned as a `fn main` local, ticked via `advance_tick`, and inspected via `entity_count`/`entity(id)`. Three deps cover the work: `rge-data`, `rge-scene-loader`, `rge-kernel-ecs` (plus `ron` for the file parse).
+- `runtime/runtime-desktop/Cargo.toml:16-19`, `runtime/runtime-mobile/Cargo.toml:16-19`, `runtime/runtime-web/Cargo.toml:16-19` — same empty `[dependencies]`; same stub `main.rs` shape — but each carries a platform substrate (winit/wgpu/wasm) that the "smallest first consumer" does not need. They are strictly larger than headless for an identical scene-load + tick demonstration.
+- `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml:16-42` already lists `rge-editor-shell`, `rge-kernel-ecs`, `rge-cad-core`, `rge-cad-projection`, `rge-io-gltf`, `rge-brep-render`, `glam`, `winit`, `tracing*`, `notify`. Adding `rge-data` + `rge-scene-loader` is small, **but** `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs:625-662` only knows how to hand a `KernelWorld` to `EditorShell::with_world_projection_graph`, which asserts on a single `BRepHandle`-carrying entity (see Q3). Wiring a generic loaded `KernelWorld` into the editor binary therefore requires modifying `crates/editor-shell` first (a separate dispatch), strictly enlarging the surface area beyond the runtime-headless option.
+- `crates/editor-shell/Cargo.toml:19-71` does not depend on `rge-data` or `rge-scene-loader`; making it the first consumer would force the editor crate to grow new deps and acquire a `Scene`-parse responsibility outside its current "host: winit lifecycle + PIE state machine" charter (`crates/editor-shell/src/lib.rs:1-3`). That is strictly larger and conceptually heavier than putting the first consumer in the runtime stub that already needs implementation per the existing TODO comment.
+
+Therefore the smallest valid first consumer is `runtime/runtime-headless` — empty current deps, zero conflict with the editor-shell PIE charter, no need to modify `rge-scene-loader` / `rge-data` / `kernel/ecs` / `editor-shell`, and an unambiguous owned-by-the-binary handoff path for the loader's `World`.
+
+**Explicit rejection of the inverse direction:** the dependency arrow `rge-data → rge-scene-loader` is rejected. It would force `crates/rge-data/Cargo.toml` to add `rge-scene-loader` (plus, transitively, `rge-components-{spatial,render,visibility}`), reversing the schema/bridge tier the W14 + ISSUE-171 dispatches established and inverting the documented module charter in `crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs:1-50` ("stateless serde + migration layer"). The first-consumer crate must depend on **both** `rge-data` and `rge-scene-loader`; neither schema- nor bridge-side crate must depend on the other in the reverse direction.
+
+### Q3. Current World Handoff API
+
+The handoff API surface that the first consumer can use **without adding a global registry** is the `rge_kernel_ecs::World` value returned by the loader itself, owned as a stack/local in the consumer binary. The relevant kernel-`World` surface already exists.
+
+Type distinction (the Planner notes require it):
+- `rge_kernel_ecs::World` — the canonical kernel ECS container. Defined at `kernel/ecs/src/world.rs:52-86`. **This is the type returned by `rge_scene_loader::load_scene_into_world(&Scene) -> Result<rge_kernel_ecs::World, SceneLoadError>`** (`crates/rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs:84`).
+- `rge_editor_shell::world::World` — a wrapper at `crates/editor-shell/src/world.rs:54-63` that **owns** a `rge_kernel_ecs::World` field (line 57) plus legacy blob storage. It exposes accessors but no constructor that absorbs a pre-built `rge_kernel_ecs::World`; it is therefore not a "receive" surface for a loader-produced kernel world (see below).
+
+Concrete surfaces on `rge_kernel_ecs::World` that produce/consume the loader's output without a global registry (every line citation taken from `kernel/ecs/src/world.rs`):
+- `pub fn new() -> Self` at `:75` — the empty-world constructor the loader itself uses internally (`crates/rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs:85`).
+- `pub fn current_tick(&self) -> u64` at `:94`.
+- `pub fn last_tick(&self) -> u64` at `:100`.
+- `pub fn advance_tick(&mut self)` at `:111-114` — increments `change_tick`, saves prior tick as `last_tick`. This is the tick API the existing golden test already pins against a loader-produced world at `crates/rge-scene-loader/tests/simple_scene.rs:110-127`.
+- `pub fn entity_count(&self) -> usize` at `:211`.
+- `pub fn entity(&self, entity: EntityId) -> Option<EntityRef<'_>>` at `:221` — used by `crates/rge-scene-loader/tests/simple_scene.rs:62-78` to assert per-entity component presence post-load.
+- `pub fn spawn_with_id(&mut self, id: EntityId)` at `:133` and `pub fn insert<C: Component>(&mut self, entity: EntityId, component: C)` at `:246` — these are the kernel APIs the loader writes through. The consumer does not call them directly, but their existence is what lets the loader return a fully-populated `World` value the consumer can simply own.
+
+These public functions on `rge_kernel_ecs::World` form a complete *produce-and-consume* surface for a loader-built world that the runtime can drive without any registry, snapshot system, or scene-graph singleton.
+
+Non-handoff surfaces that the audit must explicitly *rule out* as an integration point (each carries a hard precondition the loader cannot satisfy):
+
+- `EditorShell::with_world_projection_graph(cad_world: KernelWorld, projection: CadProjection, cad_graph: CadGraph) -> Self` at `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:638-655`. **Receives** a `KernelWorld` *by type*, but `:644-654` assert `cad_world` contains *exactly one* `BRepHandle`-carrying entity. The simple-scene loader (Transform/Camera/Light/Visibility only — `crates/rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs:32-34`) never produces `BRepHandle` entities, so this constructor would panic at handoff. It is not a viable receive API for a loader-produced world.
+- `EditorShell::with_world(mut world: World) -> Self` at `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:567-619` takes the **editor-shell wrapper** `World` (`use crate::world::World` at `:208`), *not* `rge_kernel_ecs::World`. The wrapper has no constructor that absorbs an externally built `rge_kernel_ecs::World` (its `kernel_mut()` at `crates/editor-shell/src/world.rs:102-104` only hands out a `&mut` borrow), so this is not a receive API for the loader's output without modifying editor-shell.
+- `editor_shell::world::World::kernel()` at `crates/editor-shell/src/world.rs:94-96` / `kernel_mut()` at `:102-104` — these are accessor borrows over a wrapper-owned kernel world, not handoff entry points; they cannot replace the wrapper's kernel field.
+- `runtime/runtime-{desktop,mobile,web,headless}/src/main.rs` — all four print-only stubs (each 6 lines, identical shape). They expose no current handoff API; they *are* the surface the first consumer would extend. `runtime-headless` is the smallest such candidate per Q2.
+- `kernel/app/src/app.rs:99-209` — `App` / `AppBuilder` / `App::run_frame` know nothing about `World`; they drive a phase-runner callback over `FramePhase::ALL` but never instantiate or accept a `World`. Not a handoff surface.
+
+In short: the only handoff path currently justified from current code is "loader returns `rge_kernel_ecs::World`; the consumer owns it directly." No global registry, no snapshot round-trip, no editor-shell extension required for the smallest first consumer.
+
+### Q4. Next Implementation Dispatch
+
+**ISSUE-XXX (suggested title) "runtime-headless scene-loader first consumer"** — exactly one bounded source dispatch.
+
+- **Goal (one sentence):** Wire `runtime/runtime-headless` to take a `.rge-project` path, parse it and its first referenced `.rge-scene` via `ron::from_str` into `rge_data::{Project,Scene}`, call `rge_scene_loader::load_scene_into_world(&scene)`, advance one tick on the returned `rge_kernel_ecs::World`, and print `entity_count` + `current_tick` to stdout — making `runtime-headless` the first non-test consumer of the existing `rge-scene-loader` bridge.
+- **Allowed files (the entire MAY-edit envelope):**
+  - `runtime/runtime-headless/Cargo.toml` — add exactly three workspace/path deps (`rge-data = { path = "../../crates/rge-data" }`, `rge-scene-loader = { path = "../../crates/rge-scene-loader" }`, `rge-kernel-ecs = { workspace = true }`) plus `ron = { workspace = true }`. No new workspace member, no new `[workspace.dependencies]` entry, no feature flag.
+  - `runtime/runtime-headless/src/main.rs` — replace the 4-line stub with: CLI parse of one positional `<project-path>` argument; `std::fs::read_to_string` + `ron::from_str::<rge_data::Project>` for the manifest; resolve the first `ScenePath`, `read_to_string` + `ron::from_str::<rge_data::Scene>`; `rge_scene_loader::load_scene_into_world(&scene)?`; `world.advance_tick();`; one `println!("rge-runtime-headless: loaded {} entities; current_tick = {}", world.entity_count(), world.current_tick());`. Non-zero exit on any error.
+  - `Cargo.lock` — mechanical lockfile update for the three new edges only.
+  - One `runtime/runtime-headless/tests/load_golden_simple_scene.rs` integration test that points the binary helper at `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` and asserts `entity_count == 2` and `current_tick == prior_current_tick + 1`. (Test-only; reuses the same path as `crates/rge-scene-loader/tests/simple_scene.rs:27-50`.)
+- **MUST NOT touch:** anything under `crates/rge-data/**`, `crates/rge-scene-loader/**`, `crates/editor-*/**`, `crates/asset-*/**`, `crates/io-*/**`, `crates/components-*/**`, `kernel/**`, `editor/**`, the other `runtime/runtime-{desktop,mobile,web}/**` stubs, `tools/**`, `golden-projects/**`, `.github/workflows/**`, `docs/**`, `plans/**`, ADRs, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, status files, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`, any other handoff/log artifact, the workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` block, the workspace `members` list (the crate is already a member), or any feature/lint configuration.
+- **Tests:** one integration test under `runtime/runtime-headless/tests/load_golden_simple_scene.rs` that drives the loader path against `golden-projects/simple-scene/` and asserts the entity count + tick contract — read-only against `golden-projects/**`.
+- **Verification gates (the implementation dispatch must run and document each):**
+  - `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-runtime-headless` → exit 0.
+  - `cargo build -p rge-runtime-headless` → exit 0.
+  - `cargo clippy -p rge-runtime-headless --no-deps -- -D warnings` → exit 0.
+  - `cargo test -p rge-runtime-headless --no-fail-fast` → all green.
+  - `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` → exit 0 (catches any forbidden new edge — e.g. runtime crate pulling renderer-tier deps).
+  - `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` → all green.
+  - `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0.
+- **Halt conditions (the implementation dispatch must halt rather than expand scope on any of these):**
+  - Architecture-lint rejects the `runtime-headless → rge-scene-loader` or `runtime-headless → rge-data` edges (would require an architectural decision outside this bounded dispatch).
+  - The golden-project fixture fails to parse via `ron::from_str` on the current schema (would mean a schema/migration change is required first).
+  - Any need to modify `rge-scene-loader`, `rge-data`, `editor-shell`, `editor/rge-editor`, kernel crates, component crates, or other runtime stubs.
+  - Any need to introduce a global registry, feature flag, snapshot-restore, or reflection machinery for the simple-scene fixture to load.
+  - More than one positional CLI argument or any optional flag — the smallest first consumer takes exactly one `<project-path>` arg.
+
+This dispatch is mechanically bounded by current code: every line cited in Q1-Q3 already exists, and the goal is purely additive inside one Cargo manifest + one `main.rs` + one integration test under a previously-stub crate.
+
+### Q5. Needs Human Decision
+
+`NO_NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+Q1, Q2, and Q3 are all justified from current tracked code, and they collectively bound exactly one follow-up:
+
+- Q1 establishes that no non-test parse surface exists today and that the only non-test load surface is `rge_scene_loader::load_scene_into_world` (`crates/rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs:84`).
+- Q2 names a single smallest first consumer (`runtime/runtime-headless`) — uniquely so, because every other candidate (runtime-{desktop,mobile,web}, editor/rge-editor, editor-shell) carries either a platform-substrate dep or an unsatisfied editor-shell handoff precondition (`EditorShell::with_world_projection_graph`'s single-`BRepHandle` assertion at `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:644-654`), making it strictly larger.
+- Q3 establishes that `rge_kernel_ecs::World`'s existing public surface (`new`, `spawn_with_id`/`insert` used internally, `current_tick`/`advance_tick`/`entity_count`/`entity`) is a complete produce-and-consume surface for the loader's output owned as a `fn main` local — no global registry, no editor-shell extension, no snapshot path required.
+
+Together these three pin Q4 to exactly one bounded implementation dispatch ("runtime-headless scene-loader first consumer"). No human architectural decision is needed to authorize that follow-up.

+```markdown
+## 5-Question Scene-Loader Consumer Audit Answer Block
+
+### Q1. Current Project/Scene Parse/Load Surfaces
+
+### Q2. Smallest Valid First Consumer
+
+### Q3. Current World Handoff API
+

+
+- Q1 must identify every current non-test parse or load surface found for
+  `rge_data::Project` or `rge_data::Scene`, with file/line evidence for each.
+  Treat files under `tests/` and `#[cfg(test)]` modules as test surfaces and

+  surfaces only if they clarify why they are not valid current consumers.

+  documentation comments from actual parse/load surfaces.

+  makes `rge-data` depend on `rge-scene-loader`.
+- Q3 must identify any current app/runtime/editor API surface that can receive
+  or produce a loaded `World` without adding a new global registry, with
+  file/line evidence. The answer must distinguish `rge_kernel_ecs::World` from
+  `rge_editor_shell::world::World` if both appear in the evidence.
+- Q4 must name exactly one smallest implementation dispatch only if Q1, Q2,
+  and Q3 are all justified from current code. That implementation dispatch
+  must include a one-sentence goal, allowed files, must-not-touch surfaces,
+  tests, verification gates, and halt conditions. If any of Q1-Q3 is missing
+  or ambiguous, Q4 must say `NOT APPLICABLE` and defer to Q5.
+- Q5 must return `NEEDS_HUMAN` if the first consumer, dependency direction, or
+  World handoff API is ambiguous. The answer must name the exact owner,
+  dependency, or API decision needed. If no human decision is needed, Q5 must
+  say `NO_NEEDS_HUMAN` and summarize why Q1-Q3 justify exactly one follow-up.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+

+## Constraints / Non-Goals

+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt and write an EXEC packet rather than expanding scope if
+any of the following occur:
+
+- A truthful Q1-Q5 answer requires editing any file other than the single
+  allowed EXEC report.
+- A truthful Q1-Q5 answer requires running Cargo, tests, formatters,
+  benchmarks, architecture lints, cargo-deny, `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, or a
+  speculative prototype.
+- Current tracked code does not justify exactly one smallest first consumer
+  crate for `rge-scene-loader`.
+- The only plausible dependency path would make `rge-data` depend on
+  `rge-scene-loader`.
+- Current tracked code does not expose an app/runtime/editor API surface that
+  can receive or produce a loaded `World` without adding a global registry.
+- More than one plausible first consumer or World handoff API remains after
+  current-code evidence is gathered.
+- Required evidence cannot be inspected because a required audit root is
+  missing, unreadable, or too malformed to classify.
+- Any forbidden edit or forbidden artifact appears. Stop immediately, do not
+  clean or revert it, and report the deviation.
+
+If a halt condition is hit after enough evidence has been gathered, the EXEC
+report must still include the five-question answer block to the extent
+possible. Use header `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, footer
+`HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, and `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER` when the result
+requires a human product, ownership, dependency, or API decision. Use
+`STATUS: BLOCKED`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: BLOCKED`, and
+`NEXT_ROLE: PLANNER_AI` only for a narrow missing-context blocker that the
+Planner can resolve without human architecture judgment.

+## Planner Notes
+
+The immediate goal is not to choose a preferred architecture by intuition. The
+Executor must prove from current code whether the next source dispatch is
+already mechanically bounded. Prior work intentionally kept `rge-data` as the

+this audit must preserve that direction and explicitly reject any
+`rge-data -> rge-scene-loader` dependency.
+
+Treat tests and loader-owned tests as useful evidence for behavior but not as
+valid runtime/editor consumers. Runtime stub binaries, editor app construction
+helpers, `EditorShell` constructors/accessors, and any existing World snapshot
+or handoff APIs are relevant only if they support a concrete, line-cited
+handoff path for the loader-produced `World` without introducing a global

+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is
